Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Commissioner Chan'S May 22, 2008 Order Compelling Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Aug-01-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Dean D. Paik, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Lawrence E. Porteous
Alan Harvey, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Leland F. Porteous

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Lawrence Porteous and Lawrence E. Proteous, D.D.S., Inc.'s motion for evidentiary and monetary sanctions and to compel compliance with the prior order is granted in part and denied in part. The motion for evidentiary sanctions is denied. The motion to compel compliance with the prior order and for monetary sanctions is granted. Defendant is ordered to comply with the prior order by providing the specified responses and documents within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that the answers prepared in response to the prior order are evasive in that they revised the discovery requests by adding a qualification that the requests only sought information about Vallejo patients being treated in the Vallejo office. Plaintiff's written discovery requests seek information about the status of patients after defendant had stopped working out of the Vallejo office. In addition, counsel for defendant failed to make a reasonable and good faith attempt to informally resolve the matters in dispute by failing to respond to the electronic mail message of June 16, 2008. Pursuant to subdivision (f) and (i) of Code of Civil Procedure Section 2024, monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,540 shall be paid by counsel for defendant within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Lawrence E. Porteous And Lawrence E. Porteous, D.D.S., Inc.'S Motion And Motion To Convert August 10,2007 Judgment To Money Judgment.

Ntc Of Pls' Mtn To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents Re: 1st Set Of Document Demands; Further Responses Re 1st Set Of Requests For Admission; Further Responses Re 1st Set Of Special Interrogatories; And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Dean Paik, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Lawrence E. Porteous

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Lawrence Porteous' motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents, requests for admissions, and special interrogatories is granted. The Court finds that the identity of any patients receiving treatment at the Vallejo location as well as the source and amount of any reimbursement are discoverable. Defendant may redact medical history information. Supplemental verified responses and responsive documents shall be provided within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Commissioner Bruce Chan. Not reported.
